description: Botrychium boreale, commonly called northern moonwort, is a species of fern in the family Ophioglossaceae. It has a short, single leaved rhizome that stands upright.
origin: FIN, IRK OO, ABT OO, JAP HN, EASTERN CANADA
synonyms: ["Botrychium brevifolium", "Botrychium lunaria var. boreale", "Botrychium boreale var. typicum", "Botrychium crassinervium"]

description: Botrychium lunaria is a species of fern in the family Ophioglossaceae known by the common name moonwort or common moonwort. It is the most widely distributed moonwort, growing throughout the Northern Hemisphere across Eurasia and from Alaska to Greenland, as well as temperate parts of the Southern Hemisphere.

synonyms: ["Botrychium lunaria var. lunaria", "Botrychium lunaria var. vulgare", "Botrychium lunaria var. polyphyllum", "Botrychium lunaria subsp. alpina", "Botrychium lunaria var. sinuatum", "Botrychium lunaria var. typicum", "Botrychium lunaria var. tripartitum", "Botrychium lunaria f. tripartitum", "Blechnum racemosum", "Botrychium lunatum"]
